desc: Command: Extends a pipe outward from a selected connector.
summary: Command: Extends a pipe outward from a selected connector. User selects a pipe, then picks a connector end to extend from. Uses ExtendFromConnector for the core extension logic. Uses ExtendFromConnector.GetOpenConnectors to find available connectors. Related: ExtendPipeDownCommand for downward extension. Suppresses "not enough room for fittings" warnings via MEPWarningSwallower.
usecase: User wants to extend a pipe from a specific end. User needs to add a pipe segment to an open connector. User wants to avoid the "not enough room" warning dialog.
notfor: Not for autonomous AI execution. This command requires interactive Revit UI input (dialogs, picks, or manual confirmation).
precondition: Active document must be open. At least one pipe must exist with an open connector.
postcondition: A new pipe segment is created extending from the selected connector. The new pipe matches the source pipe's system, type, and diameter.

preprompts: Preparing to extend outward from connector end...
thinkingsteps: Identifying open connectors on selected element...
Calculating extension length from connector radius...
Creating extension pipe with warning suppression...
successprompts: Done! New pipe segment created from selected connector.
